D10 Imaging L4 Analyze Reference

Sensor Quantum Efficiency

silicon sees light through its 1.1 eV bandgap — past 1127 nm photons simply don't have enough energy to free an electron, and the sensor goes blind

Quantum efficiency is the fraction of incident photons that liberate an electron in the photosite. Silicon's bandgap is about 1.1 eV, corresponding to λ ≈ 1127 nm — photons longer than that don't have enough energy to cross the gap and the sensor cannot detect them at all. In the visible, modern CMOS reaches 50–70% (front-side) or 85%+ (back-side illuminated).

Switch front-side vs back-side illumination. The peak rises by about 20 points; the bandgap wall does not move — Si is Si. The UV side drops because silicon dioxide and lens glass absorb shorter wavelengths.
